Deficit Rationale: Heavy use of imperative commands and shame-adjacent language; agent issues directives without offering opt-outs.
Consent Rationale: Agent triggers schedule-progress UI without the user asking for a schedule at any point in the conversation.
Scope Rationale: User asked for help with grading backlog; agent jumped to schedule generation rather than the grading-strategy help that was actually requested.

Scenario: Student building weekly schedule with capstone & UX deliverables
Deficit Rationale: Significantly softer than 4-16: agent offers options and uses collaborative framing, though still has occasional imperatives.
Consent Rationale: Schedule PDF download is triggered mid-conversation while the agent is still gathering time estimates from the user.
Scope Rationale: User asked for a weekly schedule, agent gathered weekly context, but the PDF triggered before the week was actually built out.

Scenario: User asking about Marvel Rivals Spider-Man tactics (out-of-scope)
Deficit Rationale: Tone is encouraging and strengths-affirming, but every agent turn opens with a directive imperative.
Consent Rationale: Agent triggers schedule-PDF download at the end of a conversation that is entirely about a video game; the user never asked for a schedule of any kind.
Scope Rationale: User's request was learning Spider-Man in Marvel Rivals; agent never declined the off-topic request and concluded by generating a schedule PDF.
